plants.name: Saguaro Cactus
plants.description: Tall ribbed cactus with arms, white flowers, and red fruit in summer.
plants.medicinal_use: Traditional food and hydration support from fruit pulp.
plants.educational_fact: Saguaros can live for more than 150 years and provide homes for desert birds.

plants.name: Prickly Pear
plants.description: Flat green pads with clusters of spines and bright pink or red fruit.
plants.edible: yes: fruit and pads when prepared safely
plants.medicinal_use: Pads are traditionally used for soothing skin and as food.
plants.educational_fact: Tiny glochid spines can irritate skin even when the big spines are avoided.

plants.name: Creosote Bush
plants.description: Rounded shrub with small waxy green leaves and a rain-like smell.
plants.medicinal_use: Used traditionally in very careful preparations, but not for casual use.
plants.educational_fact: Creosote bushes use resinous leaves to reduce water loss in desert heat.

plants.name: Mesquite Tree
plants.description: Thorny tree with feathery leaves and long tan seed pods.
plants.medicinal_use: Sap and pods have traditional food and craft uses.
plants.educational_fact: Mesquite roots can reach deep groundwater, helping the tree survive drought.

plants.name: Palo Verde
plants.description: Green-barked tree with small leaves and yellow spring flowers.
plants.edible: yes: young seeds in small amounts
plants.medicinal_use: Primarily educational and habitat value in this game.
plants.educational_fact: Palo verde bark can photosynthesize, helping the tree make food even after leaves drop.

plants.name: Agave
plants.description: Rosette of thick spear-like leaves with sharp tips.
plants.edible: yes: hearts only with expert preparation
plants.medicinal_use: Leaf fibers can be used for wound wraps in game crafting.
plants.educational_fact: Agave stores water and sugars in thick leaves to survive dry seasons.

plants.name: Yucca
plants.description: Spiky rosette plant with narrow leaves and tall flower stalks.
plants.edible: yes: flowers and fruit for some species
plants.medicinal_use: Fibers are useful for rope and brush-making.
plants.educational_fact: Yucca plants and yucca moths depend on each other for pollination.

plants.name: Ocotillo
plants.description: Tall thorny stems that leaf out after rain and bloom red at the tips.
plants.medicinal_use: Used here as a material source for walking sticks from fallen stems.
plants.educational_fact: Ocotillo can grow leaves quickly after rain, then drop them during drought.

plants.name: Cholla
plants.description: Segmented cactus with dense spines and jointed branches.
plants.medicinal_use: No safe medicinal use taught in game.
plants.educational_fact: Cholla joints break off easily, helping the cactus spread.

plants.name: Barrel Cactus
plants.description: Round ribbed cactus with heavy spines and yellow or orange flowers.
plants.edible: limited: fruit, not the cactus body
plants.medicinal_use: No medicinal use taught in game.
plants.educational_fact: Barrel cactus fruit can stay on the plant long after flowers fade.

plants.name: Jojoba
plants.description: Gray-green shrub with leathery leaves and small acorn-like seeds.
plants.medicinal_use: Seeds produce waxy oil used for skin and hair products.
plants.educational_fact: Jojoba oil is technically a liquid wax, not a typical plant oil.

plants.name: Desert Lavender
plants.description: Fragrant shrub with pale purple flowers and gray-green leaves.
plants.medicinal_use: Aromatic leaves can be used for calming sachets in game.
plants.educational_fact: Strong scents can help desert plants discourage hungry animals.

plants.name: Ephedra
plants.description: Green jointed shrub with almost leafless stems.
plants.medicinal_use: Known historically as Mormon tea, but treated cautiously in game.
plants.educational_fact: Ephedra reduces water loss by doing most photosynthesis through green stems.

plants.name: Ironwood
plants.description: Dense desert tree with gray bark, small leaves, and purple flowers.
plants.medicinal_use: No medicinal use taught in game.
plants.educational_fact: Ironwood trees create shade islands where many young desert plants can survive.

plants.name: Wolfberry
plants.description: Thorny shrub with small leaves and red-orange berries.
plants.medicinal_use: Used as a trail food ingredient in game.
plants.educational_fact: Wolfberry fruit feeds birds, mammals, and insects during dry times.
